subject: Re: Telephone call
body:
I agree it is curious.,, HOwver after describing to you my concerns, you provided to me what should have been , if I was in your shoes a document, that you could stand by and be proud of, knowing my concerns for accuracy and completeness, I was very clear that you should take as long as necessary to get it to where I could make an informed decision , as I was willing to give your firm the benefit of the doubt. . I told you I would not harp on the work previously unaccomplished, but would look to your new presentation.. I said take as long as you need , as you want a contract..  I find it remarkable , that you then propose a three year deal with a fee of 2.8 vs, 3 million, and then in the meeting your people tell me that that, is totally unrealsitic. and the time frames are inaccurate.. ,  IF I would have signed what you proposed I would have found myself at the end of our contract, with a substantial amount of work still undone.. LOgistics, which were to have been a vital
 part, totally, unthought through, and I was met with the repsonses,,, well we will figure that out.. 
----- Original Message ----
From: Jack Parker <jparker@reed-reed.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: jamerling@viaccess.net
Sent: Monday, January 8, 2007 6:15:42 AM
Subject: RE: Telephone call

Telephone call

 
DIV {
MARGIN:0px;}



i am not quite sure what might have transpired between 
wja and you during your flight to new york.  my team and i have 
worked this weekend and planned to continue for the next couple of days 
preparing the information you outlined in your email saturday morning 
(below).  we had planned to propose a meeting with you in new york on 
friday to review these budgets, schedules and analyses.

 

i must say that it is a little odd to suddenly learn 
from you that my friend and general counsel for the past 6 1/2 years is leaving 
my employment to have a relationship with you.  given the fact that he has 
been responsible for producing the information called for in our preconstruction 
services proposal and given your apparent dissatisfaction with that data, i find 
it this turn of events curious.

 

in view of the present circumstances, it does not seem 
to make sense to continue with our work.  is this your view as 
well?

 

 




From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 

Sent: Sunday, January 07, 2007 10:31 PM
To: Jack 
Parker
Cc: John Amerling
Subject: Re: Telephone 
call






I 
gave john  a ride to New York with  the intention of reviewing the 
documents you left.. Frankly I became very disturbed over how many inaccuracies, 
and omisions, were contained in the report. AS I know you wanted to secure a 
finalized contract,I am amazed that this was the level of work on which you were 
relying in order to provide me with the confidence that had been lacking.I had 
  thought  that after all our discussions that at least this time the 
numbers and schedule for the offfice would be somewhat within the bounds of 
accurate.Johm amerlin was unable to reconcile so many items that I asked how 
this was possible.. I told him in no uncertain terms that my entire staff had 
lost confidence in the ability of your firm to provide what I had clearly 
contracted for. A project of my scale  with no capital equiptment numbers 
at this time, no barging , transportation , or  various consultants 
missing,.  He had no answers except for the statement that he had decided 
he was unable to continue to work on the project. , and had decided to leave 
your firm.I like john , and immediately proposed a relationsip with him.. 



----- 
Original Message ----
From: Jack Parker <jparker@reed-reed.com>
To: 
J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com; Art Cavanagh 
<acavanagh@reed-reed.com>; John Cooney <jcooney@reed-reed.com>; 
jamerling@viaccess.net
Sent: Saturday, January 6, 2007 8:46:36 AM
Subject: 
RE: Telephone call


DIV {
MARGIN:0px;}


i will discuss with our team and by monday we 
will provide you with a schedule for these deliverables.  





From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 

Sent: Saturday, January 06, 2007 8:34 AM
To: Jack 
Parker
Cc: John Amerling; DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: Re: 
Telephone call






I 
think the only way to move forward is for your firm to   provide what 
was  clearly agreed to in the contract of feb 14 2006. Specifically an all 
in  budget for the office and schedule, ,  A target project budget( 
broken out by phases and buildings)  and schedules as deliniated in 1 e of 
the contract., this should include, but not be limited to  barging , and 
transportiation of workers,Please assume lsj is responsible for absolutely 
nothing, (i.e. insurance , transport,etc)  site lighting, infrastrucure 
enhancements, all consultants required , permitting requirements,   
Think of it as a general contractor with a fixed bid. ( understand that you will 
be conservative, but that can be handled with a flex factor of 10- 15%, i.e. 
best guess of actuals plus a specific amount of contingencies. A rough turnkey 
price for the office pool building, library complex main house roads, etc. The 
list of drop dead dates, as the contract specifies, to enable us to measure 
realistic progress  and anticipate problems, A thorough list of capital 
equiptment needed, the logistics plan for staging and storage, worker 
transport  and concrete delivery methods. A list of consultants you think 
necessary.. please include all general conditions and there cash flow 
requirements, staff housing transport etc, , also a list of work that you would 
anticipate self performing.including equiptment provided .( ie.e are we using 
your cranes and if so at what rate.). the roads, asphalt conduit for lighting 
telephone and water, ... I would appreciate you giving me a realistic sense 
of  when I can expect this, and will, after review,  either come to 
Maine to meet you and your team , or set up a meeting in new york or st, 
thomas.


http://mail.yahoo.com 










__________________________________________________
Do You Yahoo!?

http://mail.yahoo.com

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]
Sent: Sunday, January 07, 2007 10:31 PM
To: Jack Parker
Cc: John Amerling
Subject: Re: Telephone call

I gave john  a ride to New York with  the intention of reviewing the documents you left.. Frankly I became very disturbed over how many inaccuracies, and omisions, were contained in the report. AS I know you wanted to secure a finalized contract,I am amazed that this was the level of work on which you were relying in order to provide me with the confidence that had been lacking.I had   thought  that after all our discussions that at least this time the numbers and schedule for the offfice would be somewhat within the bounds of accurate.Johm amerlin was unable to reconcile so many items that I asked how this was possible.. I told him in no uncertain terms that my entire staff had lost confidence in the ability of your firm to provide what I had clearly contracted for. A project of my scale  with no capital equiptment numbers at this time, no barging , transportation , or  various consultants missing,.  He had no answers except for the statement that he had decided he was unable to continue to work on the project. , and had decided to leave your firm.I like john , and immediately proposed a relationsip with him..

To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: DKIESQ@aol.com; Art Cavanagh <acavanagh@reed-reed.com>; John Cooney <jcooney@reed-reed.com>; jamerling@viaccess.net

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]
Sent: Saturday, January 06, 2007 8:34 AM
To: Jack Parker
Cc: John Amerling; DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: Re: Telephone call

I think the only way to move forward is for your firm to   provide what was  clearly agreed to in the contract of feb 14 2006. Specifically an all in  budget for the office and schedule, ,  A target project budget( broken out by phases and buildings)  and schedules as deliniated in 1 e of the contract., this should include, but not be limited to  barging , and transportiation of workers,Please assume lsj is responsible for absolutely nothing, (i.e. insurance , transport,etc)  site lighting, infrastrucure enhancements, all consultants required , permitting requirements,   Think of it as a general contractor with a fixed bid. ( understand that you will be conservative, but that can be handled with a flex factor of 10- 15%, i.e. best guess of actuals plus a specific amount of contingencies. A rough turnkey price for the office pool building, library complex main house roads, etc. The list of drop dead dates, as the contract specifies, to enable us to measure realistic progress  and anticipate problems, A thorough list of capital equiptment needed, the logistics plan for staging and storage, worker transport  and concrete delivery methods. A list of consultants you think necessary.. please include all general conditions and there cash flow requirements, staff housing transport etc, , also a list of work that you would anticipate self performing.including equiptment provided .( ie.e are we using your cranes and if so at what rate.). the roads, asphalt conduit for lighting telephone and water, ... I would appreciate you giving me a realistic sense of  when I can expect this, and will, after review,  either come to Maine to meet you and your team , or set up a meeting in new york or st, thomas.


http://mail.yahoo.com
